records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2594 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"The two garments in which the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) entered Ihram were Yemeni cloths, one 'Abriyy and the other Zafariyy, and in these two garments, he was shrouded."
records.arabicText: 2594 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «كَانَ ثَوْبَا رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَللَّذَانِ أَحْرَمَ فِيهِمَا يَمَانِيَّيْنِ عِبْرِيٌّ وَ ظَفَارِ وَ فِيهِمَا كُفِّنَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2594 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The two garments in which the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) entered Ihram were Yemeni cloths, one 'Abriyy and the other Zafariyy, and in these two garments, he was shrouded."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2595 - Hamad narrated from Hariz, from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
"There is no harm in wearing any garment while praying."
records.arabicText: 2595 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «كُلُّ ثَوْبٍ تُصَلِّي فِيهِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تُحْرِمَ فِيهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2595 - Hamad narrated from Hariz, from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in wearing any garment while praying."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2596 - Hammad al-Nawwa’ asked, or was present when someone asked, about a Muhrim entering Ihram while wearing a striped cloak (burd).
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it. Were people not entering Ihram except in striped cloaks?"
records.arabicText: 2596 - وَ سَأَلَهُ حَمَّادٌ اَلنَّوَّاءُ أَوْ سُئِلَ وَ هُوَ حَاضِرٌ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يُحْرِمُ فِي بُرْدٍ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ وَ هَلْ كَانَ اَلنَّاسُ يُحْرِمُونَ إِلاَّ فِي اَلْبُرُودِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2596 - Hammad al-Nawwa’ asked, or was present when someone asked, about a Muhrim entering Ihram while wearing a striped cloak (burd).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in it. Were people not entering Ihram except in striped cloaks?"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2597 - Khalid ibn Abi al-'Ala' al-Khaffaf narrated:
"I saw Abu Ja'far (as) wearing a green striped cloak (burd akhdar) while he was in the state of Ihram."
records.arabicText: 2597 - وَ رَوَى خَالِدُ بْنُ أَبِي اَلْعَلاَءِ اَلْخَفَّافُ قَالَ : رَأَيْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ عَلَيْهِ بُرْدٌ أَخْضَرُ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2597 - Khalid ibn Abi al-'Ala' al-Khaffaf narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I saw Abu Ja'far (as) wearing a green striped cloak (burd akhdar) while he was in the state of Ihram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2598 - It was narrated from Amr ibn Shimmar, from his father, who said:
"I saw Abu Ja'far (as) wearing a lightweight striped cloak (burd mukhaffaf) while he was in the state of Ihram."
records.arabicText: 2598 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شِمْرٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ : رَأَيْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ عَلَيْهِ بُرْدٌ مُخَفَّفٌ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2598 - It was narrated from Amr ibn Shimmar, from his father, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I saw Abu Ja'far (as) wearing a lightweight striped cloak (burd mukhaffaf) while he was in the state of Ihram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2599 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from one of the Imams (as)  that he was asked about a man entering Ihram in a dirty garment.
Imam (as) said: "No, and I do not say it is forbidden, but what I prefer is that he purifies it. Its purification is through washing. A man should not wash the garment in which he enters Ihram until he exits Ihram, even if it becomes dirty, unless it is affected by a state of ritual impurity (janabah) or something similar, in which case he may wash it."
records.arabicText: 2599 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُحْرِمُ فِي اَلثَّوْبِ اَلْوَسِخِ فَقَالَ «لاَ وَ لاَ أَقُولُ إِنَّهُ حَرَامٌ وَ لَكِنْ أَحَبُّ ذَلِكَ إِلَيَّ أَنْ يُطَهِّرَهُ وَ طُهْرُهُ غَسْلُهُ وَ لاَ يَغْسِلُ اَلرَّجُلُ ثَوْبَهُ اَلَّذِي يُحْرِمُ فِيهِ حَتَّى يَحِلَّ وَ إِنْ تَوَسَّخَ إِلاَّ أَنْ تُصِيبَهُ جَنَابَةٌ أَوْ شَيْءٌ فَيَغْسِلَهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2599 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from one of the Imams (as)  that he was asked about a man entering Ihram in a dirty garment.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "No, and I do not say it is forbidden, but what I prefer is that he purifies it. Its purification is through washing. A man should not wash the garment in which he enters Ihram until he exits Ihram, even if it becomes dirty, unless it is affected by a state of ritual impurity (janabah) or something similar, in which case he may wash it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2600 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm if a man enters Ihram wearing a dyed or striped garment."
records.arabicText: 2600 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ اَلرَّجُلُ فِي ثَوْبٍ مَصْبُوغٍ مُمَشَّقٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2600 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if a man enters Ihram wearing a dyed or striped garment."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2601 - It was narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I heard Abu Ja'far (as) say:
"Imam Ali (as) was with some of his children when Umar passed by and said, 'What are these two dyed garments you are wearing while in the state of Ihram?'
Imam Ali (as) replied: 'We do not need anyone to teach us about the Sunnah. These two garments were dyed with clay.'"
records.arabicText: 2601 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُول : «كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَعَهُ بَعْضُ صِبْيَانِهِ فَمَرَّ عَلَيْهِ عُمَرُ فَقَالَ مَا هَذَانِ اَلثَّوْبَانِ اَلْمَصْبُوغَانِ وَ أَنْتَ مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «مَا نُرِيدُ أَحَداً يُعَلِّمُنَا بِالسُّنَّةِ إِنَّ هَذَيْنِ اَلثَّوْبَيْنِ صُبِغَا بِطِينٍ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2601 - It was narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I heard Abu Ja'far (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Imam Ali (as) was with some of his children when Umar passed by and said, 'What are these two dyed garments you are wearing while in the state of Ihram?'
Imam Ali (as) replied: 'We do not need anyone to teach us about the Sunnah. These two garments were dyed with clay.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2602 - It was narrated from Al-Husayn ibn al-Mukhtar, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "Can a man enter Ihram wearing a black garment?"
Imam (as) said: "He should not enter Ihram in a black garment, nor should a deceased person be shrouded in it."
records.arabicText: 2602 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ اَلْمُخْتَارِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَ يُحْرِمُ اَلرَّجُلُ فِي اَلثَّوْبِ اَلْأَسْوَدِ قَالَ «لاَ يُحْرِمُ فِي اَلثَّوْبِ اَلْأَسْوَدِ وَ لاَ يُكَفَّنُ فِيهِ اَلْمَيِّتُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2602 - It was narrated from Al-Husayn ibn al-Mukhtar, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "Can a man enter Ihram wearing a black garment?"
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He should not enter Ihram in a black garment, nor should a deceased person be shrouded in it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2603 - Hanan ibn Sudayr narrated:
I was sitting with Abu Abdullah (as) when a man asked him, "Can one enter Ihram wearing a garment that contains silk?"
Imam (as) called for a striped lower garment (izar furqubi) and said: "I enter Ihram in this, and it contains silk."
records.arabicText: 2603 - وَ رَوَى حَنَانُ بْنُ سَدِيرٍ قَالَ : كُنْتُ جَالِساً عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَسَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ أَ يُحْرَمُ فِي ثَوْبٍ فِيهِ حَرِيرٌ قَالَ فَدَعَا بِإِزَارٍ لَهُ فُرْقُبِيٍّ فَقَالَ «أَنَا أُحْرِمُ فِي هَذَا وَ فِيهِ حَرِيرٌ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2603 - Hanan ibn Sudayr narrated:
I was sitting with Abu Abdullah (as) when a man asked him, "Can one enter Ihram wearing a garment that contains silk?"
Imam (as) called for a striped lower garment (izar furqubi) and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I enter Ihram in this, and it contains silk."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2604 - It was narrated from Al-Halabi, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man entering Ihram wearing a garment with an embroidered border.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2604 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ اَلرَّجُلِ يُحْرِمُ فِي ثَوْبٍ لَهُ عَلَمٌ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2604 - It was narrated from Al-Halabi, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man entering Ihram wearing a garment with an embroidered border.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2605 - In a narration from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
"There is no harm if a man enters Ihram wearing an embroidered garment, but avoiding it is more beloved to me if he has the means to wear something else."
records.arabicText: 2605 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ اَلرَّجُلُ فِي اَلثَّوْبِ اَلْمُعْلَمِ وَ تَرْكُهُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ إِذَا قَدَرَ عَلَى غَيْرِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2605 - In a narration from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if a man enters Ihram wearing an embroidered garment, but avoiding it is more beloved to me if he has the means to wear something else."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2606 - Laith al-Muradi asked Imam (as) about a man entering Ihram in an embroidered garment.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, it is permissible. What is disliked is a fully patterned garment (mulham)."
records.arabicText: 2606 - وَ سَأَلَهُ لَيْثٌ اَلْمُرَادِيُّ : عَنِ اَلثَّوْبِ اَلْمُعْلَمِ هَلْ يُحْرِمُ فِيهِ اَلرَّجُلُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ إِنَّمَا يُكْرَهُ اَلْمُلْحَمُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2606 - Laith al-Muradi asked Imam (as) about a man entering Ihram in an embroidered garment.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, it is permissible. What is disliked is a fully patterned garment (mulham)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2607 - Al-Husayn ibn Abi al-‘Ala’ asked Imam (as) about a garment for a Muhrim that has been stained with saffron and then washed.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it if the scent has been removed. Even if it has been entirely dyed, as long as it leans toward whiteness and has been washed, there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2607 - وَ سَأَلَهُ اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ أَبِي اَلْعَلاَءِ : عَنِ اَلثَّوْبِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ يُصِيبُهُ اَلزَّعْفَرَانُ ثُمَّ يُغْسَلُ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ إِذَا ذَهَبَ رِيحُهُ وَ لَوْ كَانَ مَصْبُوغاً كُلُّهُ إِذَا ضُرِبَ إِلَى اَلْبَيَاضِ وَ غُسِلَ فَلاَ بَأْسَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2607 - Al-Husayn ibn Abi al-‘Ala’ asked Imam (as) about a garment for a Muhrim that has been stained with saffron and then washed.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in it if the scent has been removed. Even if it has been entirely dyed, as long as it leans toward whiteness and has been washed, there is no harm in it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2608 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If a Muhrim is compelled to wear a cloak (qabā’) made of striped cloth and cannot find any other garment, he may wear it inside out and should not insert his arms into the sleeves of the cloak."
records.arabicText: 2608 - وَ رَوَى اَلْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْجَوْهَرِيُّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِنِ اُضْطُرَّ اَلْمُحْرِمُ إِلَى أَنْ يَلْبَسَ قَبَاءً مِنْ بَرْدٍ وَ لاَ يَجِدُ ثَوْباً غَيْرَهُ فَلْيَلْبَسْهُ مَقْلُوباً وَ لاَ يُدْخِلْ يَدَيْهِ فِي يَدَيِ اَلْقَبَاءِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2608 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a Muhrim is compelled to wear a cloak (qabā’) made of striped cloth and cannot find any other garment, he may wear it inside out and should not insert his arms into the sleeves of the cloak."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2609 - It was narrated from al-Kahili, who said:
A man asked Imam (as) in my presence about a garment dyed with safflower (‘usfur) that has been washed. "Can I wear it while in the state of Ihram?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, safflower is not considered a fragrance. However, I dislike for you to wear something that makes you stand out among people."
records.arabicText: 2609 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْكَاهِلِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ وَ أَنَا حَاضِرٌ عَنِ اَلثَّوْبِ يَكُونُ مَصْبُوغاً بِالْعُصْفُرِ ثُمَّ يُغْسَلُ أَلْبَسُهُ وَ أَنَا مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ لَيْسَ اَلْعُصْفُرُ مِنَ اَلطِّيبِ وَ لَكِنِّي أَكْرَهُ أَنْ تَلْبَسَ مَا يَشْهَرُكَ بِهِ اَلنَّاسُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2609 - It was narrated from al-Kahili, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A man asked Imam (as) in my presence about a garment dyed with safflower (‘usfur) that has been washed. "Can I wear it while in the state of Ihram?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, safflower is not considered a fragrance. However, I dislike for you to wear something that makes you stand out among people."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2610 - Isma'il ibn al-Fadl asked Imam (as) about whether a Muhrim can wear a garment that has been affected by perfume.
Imam (as) said: "If the scent of the perfume has disappeared, then he may wear it."
records.arabicText: 2610 - وَ سَأَلَهُ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ اَلْفَضْلِ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ أَ يَلْبَسُ اَلثَّوْبَ قَدْ أَصَابَهُ اَلطِّيبُ فَقَالَ «إِذَا ذَهَبَ رِيحُ اَلطِّيبِ فَلْيَلْبَسْهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2610 - Isma'il ibn al-Fadl asked Imam (as) about whether a Muhrim can wear a garment that has been affected by perfume.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the scent of the perfume has disappeared, then he may wear it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2611 - It was narrated from Abu al-Hasan al-Nahdi, who said:
Sa'id al-A'raj asked Abu Abdullah (as) in my presence about a garment (khamiṣah) whose warp was made of silk (ibrīsīm) and its weft was made of goat wool (mir‘izzā).
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in entering Ihram in it. What is disliked is the garment made entirely of silk."
records.arabicText: 2611 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلنَّهْدِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلَ سَعِيدٌ اَلْأَعْرَجُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ أَنَا عِنْدَهُ عَنِ اَلْخَمِيصَةِ سَدَاهَا إِبْرِيسَمٌ وَ لَحْمَتُهَا مِرْعِزَّى فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ تُحْرِمَ فِيهَا إِنَّمَا يُكْرَهُ اَلْخَالِصُ مِنْهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2611 - It was narrated from Abu al-Hasan al-Nahdi, who said:
Sa'id al-A'raj asked Abu Abdullah (as) in my presence about a garment (khamiṣah) whose warp was made of silk (ibrīsīm) and its weft was made of goat wool (mir‘izzā).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in entering Ihram in it. What is disliked is the garment made entirely of silk."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2612 - Hammad ibn Uthman asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the khaluq of the Ka'bah and the khaluq of the grave that might be present on the Ihram garment.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in them; both are pure."
records.arabicText: 2612 - وَ سَأَلَ حَمَّادُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ خَلُوقِ اَلْكَعْبَةِ وَ خَلُوقِ اَلْقَبْرِ يَكُونُ فِي ثَوْبِ اَلْإِحْرَامِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِمَا هُمَا طَهُورَانِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2612 - Hammad ibn Uthman asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the khaluq of the Ka'bah and the khaluq of the grave that might be present on the Ihram garment.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in them; both are pure."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2613 - Sama'ah asked Imam (as) about a man whose garment is stained with the saffron of the Ka'bah while he is in the state of Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it; it is pure, so do not avoid it if it touches you."
records.arabicText: 2613 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَمَاعَةُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُصِيبُ ثَوْبَهُ زَعْفَرَانُ اَلْكَعْبَةِ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ وَ هُوَ طَهُورٌ فَلاَ تَتَّقِهِ أَنْ يُصِيبَكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2613 - Sama'ah asked Imam (as) about a man whose garment is stained with the saffron of the Ka'bah while he is in the state of Ihram.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it; it is pure, so do not avoid it if it touches you."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2614 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a Muhrim wearing a buttoned taylasan (cloak).
Imam (as) said: "Yes, as stated in the book of Imam Ali (as): 'Do not wear a taylasan until you undo its buttons.' He also said: 'It was disliked only out of concern that an ignorant person might button it while wearing it. As for someone knowledgeable, there is no harm in wearing it.'"
records.arabicText: 2614 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَلْبَسُ اَلطَّيْلَسَانَ اَلْمُزَرَّرَ قَالَ «نَعَمْ فِي كِتَابِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «لاَ تَلْبَسْ طَيْلَسَاناً حَتَّى تَحُلَّ أَزْرَارَهُ» » وَ قَالَ «إِنَّمَا كُرِهَ ذَلِكَ مَخَافَةَ أَنْ يَزُرَّهُ اَلْجَاهِلُ عَلَيْهِ فَأَمَّا اَلْفَقِيهُ فَلاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَلْبَسَهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2614 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a Muhrim wearing a buttoned taylasan (cloak).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Yes, as stated in the book of Imam Ali (as): 'Do not wear a taylasan until you undo its buttons.' He also said: 'It was disliked only out of concern that an ignorant person might button it while wearing it. As for someone knowledgeable, there is no harm in wearing it.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2615 - Rifa’ah ibn Musa asked Imam (as) about a Muhrim wearing socks (jawrabayn).
Imam (as) said: "Yes, and also leather slippers (khuffayn) if he is compelled to wear them."
records.arabicText: 2615 - وَ سَأَلَهُ رِفَاعَةُ بْنُ مُوسَى : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَلْبَسُ اَلْجَوْرَبَيْنِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ اَلْخُفَّيْنِ إِذَا اُضْطُرَّ إِلَيْهِمَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2615 - Rifa’ah ibn Musa asked Imam (as) about a Muhrim wearing socks (jawrabayn).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, and also leather slippers (khuffayn) if he is compelled to wear them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2616 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Ja'far (as) regarding a Muhrim wearing leather slippers (khuff) if he does not have sandals.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, but he must cut open the top of the foot area. A Muhrim may also wear a cloak (qabā’) if he does not have a cloak-like garment (rida’), but he should wear it inside out."
records.arabicText: 2616 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَلْبَسُ اَلْخُفَّ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ نَعْلٌ قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ لَكِنْ يَشُقُّ ظَهْرَ اَلْقَدَمِ وَ يَلْبَسُ اَلْمُحْرِمُ اَلْقَبَاءَ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ رِدَاءٌ وَ يَقْلِبُ ظَهْرَهُ لِبَاطِنِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2616 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Ja'far (as) regarding a Muhrim wearing leather slippers (khuff) if he does not have sandals.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Yes, but he must cut open the top of the foot area. A Muhrim may also wear a cloak (qabā’) if he does not have a cloak-like garment (rida’), but he should wear it inside out."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2617 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Do not wear a garment with buttons while you are in the state of Ihram unless you wear it inside out. Do not wear a garment that is worn like a tunic (tadarru’), nor trousers (sarawil), unless you have no loincloth (izar). Do not wear leather slippers (khuffayn) unless you have no sandals (na'lan)."
records.arabicText: 2617 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ تَلْبَسْ ثَوْباً لَهُ أَزْرَارٌ وَ أَنْتَ مُحْرِمٌ إِلاَّ أَنْ تَنْكُسَهُ وَ لاَ ثَوْباً تَدَرَّعُهُ وَ لاَ سَرَاوِيلَ إِلاَّ أَنْ لاَ يَكُونَ لَكَ إِزَارٌ وَ لاَ خُفَّيْنِ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَكَ نَعْلاَنِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2617 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not wear a garment with buttons while you are in the state of Ihram unless you wear it inside out. Do not wear a garment that is worn like a tunic (tadarru’), nor trousers (sarawil), unless you have no loincloth (izar). Do not wear leather slippers (khuffayn) unless you have no sandals (na'lan)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2618 - Zurara narrated from one of the Imams (as), who said:
I asked him about what is disliked for a Muhrim to wear.
Imam (as) said: "He may wear any garment except a single garment worn like a tunic (tadarru’)."
records.arabicText: 2618 - وَ رَوَى زُرَارَةُ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَمَّا يُكْرَهُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ أَنْ يَلْبَسَهُ فَقَالَ «يَلْبَسُ كُلَّ ثَوْبٍ إِلاَّ ثَوْباً وَاحِداً يَتَدَرَّعُهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2618 - Zurara narrated from one of the Imams (as), who said:
I asked him about what is disliked for a Muhrim to wear.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He may wear any garment except a single garment worn like a tunic (tadarru’)."
